meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no coding experience. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Email Automation for Non-Coders (SaaS Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop manual work. Learn how to build scalable email automation for your SaaS without coding experience using Antigravity and Claude Code. Start today.
content.answer_block: You don't need to be a developer to build production-grade email automation. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent implementation, you can deploy a robust, event-driven email system that triggers based on user behavior, directly converting prospects into paying customers.
content.intro:
The cold reality of a founder’s life is that manual outreach is a death sentence for growth. Every hour you spend copy-pasting emails is an hour you aren't shipping features or closing deals. The technical barrier—the feeling that you need a CS degree to automate your communication—is the single greatest bottleneck to your revenue.

But the game has changed. You no longer need to write boilerplate from scratch. By combining the architectural speed of Antigravity with the reasoning capabilities of Claude Code, you can transform your manual workflow into a self-sustaining engine. This guide is your blueprint to bypass the dev cycle and launch your automation stack today.

steps.title: Define the Trigger-Action Schema
steps.description: Identify the exact user event (e.g., sign-up, abandoned cart) that initiates the email flow. Clarity here prevents feature bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema definition for an email automation service that accepts event payloads (user_id, event_type, timestamp) and maps them to specific email templates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file schema.json --context 'define event-driven architecture for transactional emails'

steps.title: Configure the Email Provider API
steps.description: Integrate with a reliable provider like Postmark or SendGrid. We focus on low-latency delivery and high inbox placement.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a boilerplate integration module for Postmark API that handles template rendering and batch sending with error logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --package postmark --instruction 'implement modular email sender with logging'

steps.title: Develop the Logic Engine
steps.description: Build the brain that decides which user gets what email based on business rules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust controller that processes incoming webhooks, validates user data, and executes conditional logic to select the correct email template.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file logic.js --prompt 'create a conditional dispatcher for user onboarding sequences'

steps.title: Implement Resilience and Retries
steps.description: Production systems fail; your automation must be self-healing. We implement exponential backoff for failed deliveries.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a queue-based retry mechanism using a simple local store or Redis to handle API failures during email dispatching.
steps.claude_code_command: claude update --file worker.js --instruction 'add exponential backoff retry logic for API calls'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Containerize your logic and push it to a cloud environment to ensure it runs 24/7 without intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a production-ready Dockerfile and deployment script for a Node.js-based email automation microservice.

pro_tips: Always use transactional email providers rather than your personal SMTP to avoid the spam folder trap.
Implement a 'circuit breaker' pattern in your logic to stop all outgoing emails if your bounce rate exceeds 2%.
Keep your email templates as separate JSON files; this allows you to update marketing copy without touching the core codebase.
Log every single email event to a database; you will need this data for auditing and re-engagement campaigns later.
Never send emails directly from the main thread; always push tasks to an asynchronous queue to keep your app fast.
content.next_steps: Once your automation is live, focus on A/B testing your subject lines. Use the data you've logged to identify where users are dropping off in your funnel and adjust your email sequence accordingly.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting technical hurdles dictate your revenue potential. You have the tools, the blueprint, and the strategy to build a world-class automation stack. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
